mentions.raw_name: Kraken banner (golden kraken on black field)
Ermine cloak with silver wolf's head clasp
Ramsay's men / Bastard's Boys (Damon Dance-for-Me, Skinner, Sour Alyn, Yellow Dick, Grunt)
Crannogmen / bog devils (Fenn, Reed, Peat, Boggs, Cray, Quagg, Greengood, Blackmyre)
The Drowned God (referenced — Reek suggests giving the dead to the Drowned God)
The storm god (depicted on Ralf Kenning's shield)
Unnamed gods ("The gods are laughing" — Ramsay)
The Faith of the Seven (implied — "Seven is a sacred number")
Crannogmen / bog devils / swamp-dwellers / frog-eaters / mud-men
Ramsay's letter/parchment with pink wax seal (safe conduct)
Ralf Kenning's shield (storm god's cloudy hand with lightning)
Reek's black leather gloves (stuffed with wool)
Enclosed wagon with dark blue velvet curtains
Ermine cloak with silver wolf's head clasp
Decoy armor (dark grey plate, blood-red leather tunic, screaming-head rondels, pink cloak with blood droplets, closed helm with red silk streamers)
Blood-bloom flowers (pale petals growing from corpses)
Poisoned arrows of the crannogmen (shafts smeared with shit and worse; caused Ralf Kenning's fatal infection despite treatment with boiling wine)
Blood-bloom flowers (pale flowers growing from corpses' wounds)
The hammer of the waters (legend referenced — children of the forest called it down at the Children's Tower to break the lands of Westeros in two)
War of the Five Kings (implicit — Robb's campaign south, the current military situation)
Greyjoy's Rebellion (referenced — "Lord Balon bent the knee when Robert broke his wall")
The kingsmoot (referenced — crowned Euron over Victarion)
Siege/defense of Moat Cailin (the ironborn garrison's prolonged holding action)
Lord of the Hornwood (Ramsay Bolton's title)
Prince (Theon's claimed rank among the ironborn)
Captain (unnamed — the one who put Kenning in command)
Children of the forest (referenced in legend — called down the hammer of the waters at Moat Cailin)
Lizard lions (fauna of the Neck — teeth like daggers)
Ghostskin (white slimy streamers on Moat Cailin's walls)
Rock wife / salt wives (ironborn marriage customs — Adrack Humble has one rock wife and three salt wives)
Kingsmoot (ironborn custom for choosing a king)
